title: Erythrostemon laxus
taxonomy: Erythrostemon laxus is classified in the family Apocynaceae. This family is part of the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, it belongs to the clade Angiosperms. The clade Angiosperms is further divided into the clade Eudicots, which is the most recent classification for Erythrostemon laxus. This classification places it among the flowering plants.

intro: Erythrostemon laxus, also known as the Mexican palo verde, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erythrostemon laxus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
